cases.title: Light Mode Uses A Single Checkpoint
cases.description: Verify a light-engagement project can record a meaningful note, step completion, and handoff in one checkpoint call instead of multiple small writes.
cases.summary: The light-engagement project used forge_checkpoint to capture one note, one phase step, and one handoff update in a single call.

checks.description: the small project starts in light mode before the checkpoint runs
forge_checkpoint can update both memory and phases in one call
the checkpoint result reports both the logged note and completed step
the checkpoint writes the updated handoff without needing a separate session-end call
the resulting state shows a real checkpoint note and a completed phase step rather than scattered small writes

artifacts.label: checkpoint output

cases.title: Resume Diff And File Links Stay Actionable
cases.description: Simulate a small continuity-heavy session that links state to files, then verify forge_load surfaces both the changed-since-last-session summary and the linked-file map.
cases.summary: forge_load linked active continuity state back to the affected files, showed which records own each link, flagged missing files, and summarized what changed since the previous session boundary.

checks.description: workingView exposes a changed-since-last-session summary when new state has been written
resumeDiff highlights new issues and drift since the previous session boundary
resumeDiff includes the files touched by the changed continuity records
workingView aggregates multiple reasons against the same linked file
linkedFiles include owner metadata so the agent can see which issue, phase, or step points at a file
linkedFiles expose the primary active phase owner so the agent can tell which incomplete phase owns a file right now
linkedFiles preserve multiple active phase owners when a file spans the current phase and a later incomplete phase
linkedFiles distinguish real local files from stale or not-yet-created file links
high-severity linked drift can surface as a scope-shift signal in the resume diff
workingView warns when continuity-linked files are missing locally

cases.title: Medium Fixture Partial Resume Flow
cases.description: Leave a medium-sized project in a partially progressed state, close the session, reload continuity, and resume from the saved handoff.
cases.summary: The medium fixture preserved a partial handoff, resumed from that handoff, then completed the active issue and phase without losing continuity.

checks.description: the medium fixture starts with a usable session handoff
session_end writes the new partial-progress next step before the resume load
the newly promoted medium phase stays visible when work resumes
resuming the workflow resolves the open issue and removes it from openIssues
the original active phase completes after the resumed step is marked done
the medium resume scenario remains fast enough for local iterative work

cases.title: Large Fixture Resume After Partial Mutation
cases.description: Mutate a larger project across issues, drift, and phases, persist a handoff midstream, then resume and finish the remaining critical work.
cases.summary: The larger workflow survived a partial stop, then resumed into issue resolution, drift acknowledgement, and active-phase completion with the handoff intact.

checks.description: the large fixture resumes from the new saved next step instead of the old one
the reopened issue re-enters the open issue list during the partial-progress resume
the large resume flow can close the reopened issue cleanly on the second pass
acknowledged critical drift remains visible and attention-worthy after the resume pass
the partially active large phase completes after the resumed mutation sequence
the larger resume scenario stays within a reasonable local stress budget

cases.title: End-to-End Continuity Stress Run
cases.description: Initialize a fresh project, record multiple events, resolve an issue, progress phases, close the session, and verify the resumed continuity view.
cases.summary: The end-to-end run stayed coherent across initialization, mutation, drift, phase progression, and session resume.

checks.description: final forge_load confirms the project is now Forge-managed
the final working view carries forward the session handoff
the contradiction remains visible in openDrift after the stress run
the rebuilt phase is fully completed by the end of the run
the final working view still carries a recent decision and observation
resolved issues remain out of the final open issue list
the end-to-end stress run completes within a reasonable local duration
the final forge_load payload stays within a reasonable benchmark size
